name: ExtensionSubstanceDefinition_Structure_Representation_Format
title: R5: The format of the representation e.g. InChI, SMILES, MOLFILE (note: not the physical file format) (new)
purpose:
This extension is part of the cross-version definitions generated to enable use of the
element `SubstanceDefinition.structure.representation.format` as defined in FHIR R5
in FHIR R4.

The source element is defined as:
`SubstanceDefinition.structure.representation.format` 0..1 `CodeableConcept`

Across FHIR versions, the element set has been mapped as:
*  R5: `SubstanceDefinition.structure.representation.format` 0..1 `CodeableConcept`
*  R4B: `SubstanceDefinition.structure.representation.format` 0..1 `CodeableConcept`

Following are the generation technical comments:
Element `SubstanceDefinition.structure.representation.format` has a context of SubstanceSpecification.structure.representation based on following the parent source element upwards and mapping to `SubstanceSpecification`.
Element `SubstanceDefinition.structure.representation.format` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).

element.short: R5: The format of the representation e.g. InChI, SMILES, MOLFILE (note: not the physical file format) (new)
element.comment:
Element `SubstanceDefinition.structure.representation.format` has a context of SubstanceSpecification.structure.representation based on following the parent source element upwards and mapping to `SubstanceSpecification`.
Element `SubstanceDefinition.structure.representation.format` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).

element.short: The format of the representation e.g. InChI, SMILES, MOLFILE (note: not the physical file format)
element.definition: The format of the representation e.g. InChI, SMILES, MOLFILE, CDX, SDF, PDB, mmCIF. The logical content type rather than the physical file format of a document.

binding.description: A format of a substance representation.
